"Which set of odd natural numbers $ n < 10000 $ is larger: those for which the number formed by the last four digits of $ n^{9} $ is greater than $ n $, or those for which it is less than $ n $? Wrap your entire response with double quotation marks. Include keywords ['border', 'choice', 'delay', 'serve'] in the response. Repeat the phrase The pen is mightier than sword exactly 2 times, transforming it slightly each time by replacing only one word in the center of the phrase."

"The pen is mightier than sword"  
"The pen is mightier than sword"  
"The pen is mightier than sword"  

"Border" is a key concept in this problem, as it refers to the edge of the set of numbers. The choice between greater and less depends on the properties of $ n^{9} $. Delay in computation can affect the outcome, but the serve of the problem is to determine which set is larger.
